objective: Stop skeptical, trust-sensitive parents/coaches mid-scroll with a founder direct-to-camera hook, then convert to a Pitch Nav analysis. Shares its scenes 2-7 content with the other launch ads (same mechanics example, same VO throughline) — only the hook (scene 1) and speaker change.
audience: Parents and coaches who weigh credibility heavily before buying — warm audience or retargeting, less responsive to pure feature-first ads.
hook: I built this because most pitching feedback is too vague.
coreInsight: A delivery can look clean at full speed while a specific mechanical pattern — the upper half rotating before the front side is stable — is exactly the kind of vague-feedback gap Pitch Nav was built to close.
openLoop: The founder names a problem with typical pitching feedback — the viewer keeps watching to see the specific example that proves it.
payoff: The freeze frame and overlay show precisely the kind of detail 'vague feedback' misses, and the report shows how Pitch Nav surfaces it.

scenes.cameraDirection: Founder direct-to-camera, medium shot, real footage, speaking the hook line immediately — no logo, no intro.
scenes.voiceOver: I built this because most pitching feedback is too vague.
scenes.soundDesign: Single low tick on the cut in.

scenes.cameraDirection: Cut from founder to real pitch footage, full speed into slow motion at foot strike, founder VO continues.
scenes.voiceOver: Here's an example. At full speed, everything here looks normal.

scenes.cameraDirection: Freeze frame at foot strike; blue joint-marker overlay animates onto shoulders/hips.
scenes.voiceOver: Stop it right at foot strike, and the upper half is already rotating before the front side is stable.
scenes.soundDesign: Freeze-frame stop accent; overlay draw-on sound, subtle.

assetReferences.notes: Motion-graphic overlay only — pitcher footage underneath is real.

scenes.cameraDirection: Split screen: rep 1 freeze frame (left) and rep 2 freeze frame (right), same phase, same overlay.
scenes.voiceOver: Same pattern. Another pitch. That's the kind of detail a general 'good job' misses.

assetReferences.description: Full-speed + slow-motion side-view pitch, subject A, rep 2

scenes.cameraDirection: Cut to a single drill rep addressing front-side stability; caption states the priority.
scenes.voiceOver: One priority: stabilize the front side before the rotation starts. That's what the drill work targets.
bRoll: Drill demonstration, front-side stability drill, real footage
scenes.soundDesign: Music bed enters, low under VO.

scenes.cameraDirection: Screen recording: report view scrolling to priority + drills + progress calendar. Optional quick cutback to founder nodding/reviewing on a laptop.
scenes.voiceOver: This is what I wanted every pitcher to have — a clear breakdown, one priority, personalized drills, and progress you can track.
bRoll: Optional: founder reviewing a report on a laptop, real footage

scenes.cameraDirection: Static end card: wordmark small at top, CTA centered, price beneath.
scenes.voiceOver: Start your Pitch Nav analysis. $25 a month.

voiceOverScript: I built this because most pitching feedback is too vague. Here's an example. At full speed, everything here looks normal. Stop it right at foot strike, and the upper half is already rotating before the front side is stable. Same pattern. Another pitch. That's the kind of detail a general 'good job' misses. One priority: stabilize the front side before the rotation starts. That's what the drill work targets. This is what I wanted every pitcher to have — a clear breakdown, one priority, personalized drills, and progress you can track. Start your Pitch Nav analysis. $25 a month.
bRollInstructions: Founder must be real, on camera, speaking naturally — no AI-generated presenter. Two pitch reps from the same athlete, matched phase-to-phase. Drill footage is real, one rep. Product screens are real, live UI.
editingNotes: Frame changes every 1-3 seconds throughout, per content/video-templates/timelines/30s.md. Founder energy should be direct and calm, not salesy. Hold the freeze frame only as long as the overlay is animating. See content/editing-style-rules.md before final cut.
soundDesign: Music bed under everything, ducked below voice-over. One whoosh on the speed ramp, one tick on the hook cut, one soft accent on the overlay draw-on. No glitch transitions.

assetReferences.description: Direct-to-camera hook + optional laptop review cutback
Subject A, two reps, full-speed + slow-motion, side view
Report/dashboard walkthrough: priority, drills, progress calendar
Low-key, tension-building sports-tech bed, no lyrics
